t_ch: 至于技术部分，老希可不太懂，您得去问工匠们，三两句话解释不清楚…而且，哪怕现在，这神体也还藏着不少秘密咧！就比如说，那「翻飞试炼」——
t_en: When it comes to the technical stuff, Old Hygilos isn't really in the know — you'd be better off checking with the craftsmen. It's too complex to explain... And even now, the corpus of this deity still harbors plenty of secrets! Take the Trial of Whimsy, for instance...

t_ch: 收集长了一副怪脸的硬币就能拿到宝贝，而且宝贝本身也都是些个怪玩意，更别提那硬币还跟扎格列斯老爷同名？怪到泰坦家里去嘞。
t_en: Collecting those funny-faced coins earns you treasures, which are themselves odd bits and bobs. And what's more, they're named after the Great Zagreus? It's the oddest of oddities.

t_ch: 为什么会选择开店？
t_en: Why did you choose to set up a shop?

t_ch: 哎哟，看来客人您对我这独到的商业眼光很是好奇啊。但实话实说，我这铺子开了十年有余，开始是借别人的点子，后来才一步步转了业务。
t_en: Oho, it appears that you are very curious about my unique business acumen. To be honest, though, this shop has been open for about a decade. This was someone else's idea at first, but I later shifted my business focus.

t_ch: 这行业的源头，是以前扎格列斯老爷还会显灵的时候，信徒们发明出来的经济模式，为那些生来就懒的兄弟求个福祉。
t_en: The origin of this industry was a business model invented by Great Zagreus' disciples, way back when the Titan still manifested themself, providing an easy means of prospering for those inherently indolent.

t_ch: 那些兄弟，虽说肚子里有三两学识，但就是不愿找个营生。偏偏又想赚利衡币，就只好去打劫，不是，去路上跟人交流。但这抢…这聊天可是门学问。
t_en: Those brethren, though possessed of smarts, were reluctant to engage in honest work. They wanted to earn Balance Coins, so they had to resort to robbery, er, I mean chatting with people on the streets. But rob... chatting was an art form by themselves.

t_ch: 于是「翻飞基金」就成立了。人们可以给兄弟投一笔利衡币，他们会用来添置抢劫，不是，交流用的设备。最后不管抢到…聊出来什么东西，都会拿去给金主算分成。
t_en: And so the Whimsy Fund was founded. People could invest Balance Coins in the brethren, and they would use the money to fuel the device for robbery, I mean, communication. No matter what they ro... received through communication, the proceeds would be split with the investor.

t_ch: 天才。
t_en: Genius.

t_ch: 见鬼。
t_en: Heinous.

t_ch: 当然，最后这分成能不能到手，还得看兄弟们的意愿…正可谓高风险高回报。可惜可惜，没多久，这行业就被塔兰顿的腿子们一锅端了。
t_en: Of course, whether the commission would be gained was still at our fellows' whims in the end... With high risks come rich rewards, as they say. A pity though that soon this industry was shut down by Talanton's thugs.

t_ch: 那时候，咱正巧听到工匠们给这手琢磨透了，我就偷…借了一点经验。
t_en: At that time, I happened to overhear that the craftsmen had completely figured it all out about this Hand, so I stole... borrowed some experience.

t_ch: 让这神器和翁法罗斯最先进的经营模式结合，这铺子就这么成了，贼赚！
t_en: By combining this relic with Amphoreus' most advanced business model, this shop was born — criminally profitable!

t_ch: 你这还有其他业务吗？
t_en: Do you have other businesses?

t_ch: 多的很呐！店里摆着些借翻飞之手从遗迹挖出来的老古董。有些还残留有其他泰坦的权能，简直就是宝贝库——虽然多数不知道有什么用，你得碰运气。
t_en: Plenty! The antiques displayed around the shop were all excavated from ruins by the Whimsy Hand. Some of them even retain vestiges of the other Titans' authority. It's a veritable vault of treasures. Though many of their uses are unknown — you'll have to try your luck.

t_ch: 此外呢，最受欢迎的服务是「代辩」以及「真言狮口代答」，老希亲自出马，保准变着法驳倒对方，让您能获得充足的面子！
t_en: Other than that, our most popular services are "substitute debaters" and "acting Verax Leo responders." I will personally step in and ensure the opponent is left tongue-tied with my eloquence, leaving you to reap all the ensuing respect and admiration!

t_ch: 老希的格言就是——「利衡币够，天长地久」！只要有需求，记得来咱这铺子打听打听，老希负责把您的利衡币变成解决问题的思路！
t_en: My mantra is — with enough Balance Coins, everything lasts forever! If you've got a request, be sure to swing by to check us out! I will guarantee your hard-earned Balance Coins turned into solutions!

t_ch: 告辞。
t_en: Goodbye.

t_ch: 得嘞，朋友，常来光顾哈？
t_en: Be well, friend. Stop by often, eh?

t_ch: 告辞。
t_en: Goodbye.

t_ch: 得嘞，朋友，常来光顾哈？
t_en: Be well, friend. Stop by often, eh?

t_ch: 这位客人，在浴场购买秘酿的免责协议签署了吗？
t_en: Hello, did you sign the liability waiver for purchasing ambrosia at the bath?

t_ch: 什么秘酿？
t_en: What ambrosia?

t_ch: 什么免责协议？
t_en: What liability waiver?

t_ch: 签…签了！
t_en: I... I did!

t_ch: 事关个人安全，可不能疏忽。想要畅饮浴场特质秘酿，一定得仔细看过协议——
t_en: We can never be too careful when it comes to personal safety... To quench your thirst via our bath's craft ambrosia, please read the agreement carefully...

t_ch: 这是什么奇怪的习俗？
t_en: What weird custom is this?

t_ch: 你已踏入「满溢之杯」法吉娜的地盘。在享乐之神的浴场，唯有豪饮与纵情被视为对它的尊敬，我们也乐在其中。
t_en: You have entered the domain of Phagousa, the Chalice of Plenty. While you bask in their divine bath, only indulgence and revelry are considered acts of respect to them, and thus we, too, shall revel in such.

t_ch: 我海量！
t_en: I've got a sailor's thirst!

t_ch: 你已踏入「满溢之杯」法吉娜的地盘。在享乐之神的浴场，唯有豪饮与纵情被视为对它的尊敬，而这份虔诚你不一定承受得住。
t_en: You have entered the domain of Phagousa, the Chalice of Plenty. While you bask in their divine bath, only indulgence and revelry are considered acts of respect to them, and you may be unable to bear the weight of this devotion.

t_ch: 你负责把人扔出去？
t_en: You in charge of tossing people out?

t_ch: 没错。在享乐之神法吉娜的地盘，唯有豪饮与纵情被视为对它的尊敬，而这份虔诚你不一定承受得住。
t_en: That is correct. While you bask in Phagousa's domain, only indulgence and revelry are considered acts of respect to them, and you may be unable to bear the weight of this devotion.

t_ch: 我所售的秘酿瓶口紧封，瓶身别无二致。饮用方式也有规矩，必须揭开木塞后一饮而尽，等待「满溢之杯」的平静或激荡。如此放弃理性的抉择正是它乐于见到的……
t_en: The ambrosia I sell is tightly sealed, its bottle unremarkable. There are also rules to drinking it. After uncorking the bottle, you must down it in one go, then await the calming or stirring of the Chalice of Plenty. Such a decision in abandoning your inhibitions is what the Titan delights in...

t_ch: …它的意志掩藏在迷醉之中，令每人的觐见旅途不尽相同——或镇定或激荡，脑中闪过智慧还是鲁莽，完成不可能之事甚至直面它本人的事迹亦有记载……
t_en: ...Their will is hidden within inebriation, and the journey each is subjected to is unique — calm or otherwise, whether it is wisdom or fervor that flashes through the mind. There have even been accounts of accomplishing impossible tasks, and even coming face to face with Phagousa themself...

t_ch: 老板，来瓶秘酿！
t_en: A bottle of ambrosia please!

t_ch: 真有这么神奇？
t_en: Is it truly that amazing?

t_ch: 包见到法吉娜吗？
t_en: I'll definitely be able to see Phagousa?

t_ch: 呃…其实刚刚所说的迷醉体验…也都是他人向我转述的。我生来便遭法吉娜大人忽视，至今从未迷醉过。
t_en: Err... these intoxicated experiences I have described... were what others have told me. I have been ignored by Phagousa all my life and never had the chance at the experience.

t_ch: …不过，我仍尝试在他人的故事中感受法吉娜的奇迹，试图拼凑出它的模样。
t_en: ...But still, I try to vicariously experience Phagousa's miracles through the stories of others, to piece together the Titan's visage.

t_ch: 若你愿意将你的迷醉之感转告我，我会非常感激的。为表示诚意，第一杯秘酿的费用由我承担。请享受这份祝福吧！
t_en: If you're willing to relay your inebriated journey to me, I would truly appreciate it. As a token of gratitude, this first cup of ambrosia is on the house. Please enjoy!

t_ch: 需要畅饮秘酿吗？
t_en: Need an ambrosia?

t_ch: 开始畅饮！
t_en: Let the party begin!

t_ch: 请！别忘了畅饮的步骤——揭开木塞，一饮而尽。
t_en: Please! Do not forget the proper drinking etiquette — uncork, and down in one sitting.

t_ch: 好的，收你<unbreak>300</unbreak>利衡币。
t_en: All right, I'll take <unbreak>300</unbreak> Balance Coins.

t_ch: 感谢你贡献的迷醉逸闻！我感觉离法吉娜大人又近了一步。
t_en: Thank you for contributing your intoxicated anecdote! I feel one more step closer to Phagousa.

t_ch: 秘酿是如何制作的？
t_en: How is ambrosia made?

t_ch: 我们前往海岸，取海水作为基底，盛入特殊的容器中。一日中不同时段的海水能为秘酿带来不同的风味——
t_en: We head to the coast, collect seawater to create its base, then pour it into a special vessel. Seawater collected at different times of the day applies different flavors to the ambrosia...

t_ch: 门扉时的海水清新如鲜果，明晰时饱含花香，践行时如燧石，离愁时最干涩，幕匿时又有回甘。
t_en: Seawater collected during the Entry Hour tastes like fresh fruit, Lucid Hour's has floral notes, and the Action Hour tastes like flint. Seawater during the Parting Hour tastes the driest, but the Curtain-Fall Hour has a lingering sweetness.

t_ch: 再之后，法吉娜的信徒会敬拜今日取回的海水，就像敬拜法吉娜大人一般。祭司带领我们念诵它的密语，咏唱着它谱写的歌谣。
t_en: Then, Phagousa's disciples will worship the collected seawater, just as they worship the great Phagousa. The priests guide us to recite their hidden words, chanting the hymns they compose.

t_ch: 海水被赐予「满溢之杯」的神力就成了秘酿，这份神力让畅饮者迷醉于大海的波涛与漩涡中。
t_en: The seawater infused with the divine power from the Chalice of Plenty turns into ambrosia, able to intoxicate those who drink it with surging waves and swirling currents of the sea.

t_ch: 迷醉最接近法吉娜大人存在的体验。作为法吉娜的信徒，无法感受迷醉是一种失格。我一定是过去无意间冒犯了它，它才对我闭门不见……
t_en: Intoxication is the closest one can get to experiencing the great Phagousa. As one of Phagousa's disciples, being unable to experience such intoxication is a failure. I must have offended them in the past, and that's why they turn a blind eye...

t_ch: 法吉娜大人，你掀起波澜，抛弃了我……
t_en: O Great Phagousa, you have churned the tides and abandoned me...

t_ch: 太不公平~
t_en: How unfair!

t_ch: 哎，我只能安慰自己，这份永恒的清醒能让我更好服务迷醉的客人，也算是对法吉娜大人的一种侍奉了。
t_en: *sigh* I can only comfort myself with the thought that this eternal sobriety helps me better serve inebriated guests — a form of servitude to Phagousa, at least.

t_ch: 搞不好你有法吉娜的血统。
t_en: Who knows, you might have Phagousa's bloodline.

t_ch: 你的意思是…就像法吉娜不会迷醉于自己的神力一样…体内流淌着法吉娜血脉的人理应也不会……
t_en: You mean... just like how Phagousa does not get drunk over their own divinity... those whose bodies course with Phagousa's blood technically also won't...

t_ch: 太荒谬了！我并非什么高贵之人，若如你所说…这简直是对法吉娜大人的一种玷污！
t_en: Preposterous! I am not someone so noble. If it is as you say... it would be an absolute affront to Phagousa's repute!

t_ch: 哎，我只能安慰自己，这份永恒的清醒能让我更好服务迷醉的客人，也算是对法吉娜大人的一种侍奉了。
t_en: *sigh* I can only comfort myself with the thought that this eternal sobriety helps me better serve inebriated guests — a form of servitude to Phagousa, at least.

t_ch: 我再想想。
t_en: Let me think about it.

t_ch: 小店随时欢迎！
t_en: You're welcome any time!

t_ch: 先喝一口…
t_en: Try a sip...

t_ch: 你捧着瓶身，小心翼翼尝了一口……
t_en: You lift the bottle and cautiously take a sip...

t_ch: 有点上头…
t_en: It's a little strong...

t_ch: 感觉还不够…
t_en: I need more...

t_ch: 你很勇敢，再来一口……
t_en: What courage! Try another sip...

t_ch: 干了！
t_en: Bottoms up!

t_ch: 你捧起瓶身，一饮而尽……
t_en: You raise the bottle, and down it in one go...

t_ch: 你捧起瓶身，一饮而尽……
t_en: You raise the bottle, and down it in one go...

t_ch: 秘酿淡雅的风味滑过舌尖，海面波光粼粼——
t_en: The subtle flavors of the ambrosia glide across your tongue, the sea's surface a glistening crystalline...

t_ch: …入口有非常明确的泥土、青草、风信子的香气……
t_en: ...Distinct earthy, grassy, and hyacinth aromas on the palate...

t_ch: …这风味让你陷入幻想，画面逐渐浮现——你是一棵盛开的风信子，智慧花园惬意的暖风吹拂，心情也变得愉悦……
t_en: ...This flavor draws you into a dreamy fantasy, its visuals gradually emerging — you are a hyacinth in full bloom, basking 'neath the warm breeze in the Garden of Wisdom, your mood uplifted...

t_ch: 此时一只横冲直撞的小奇美拉朝你飞奔过来。它在你面前一个急刹，开始原地打转，急不可耐——
t_en: At this moment, a tiny, reckless chimera lunges at you. It abruptly breaks in front of you and starts spinning in circles, bursting with impatience...

t_ch: 停下，这里不是厕所啊——！！！
t_en: Stop, this isn't a toilet!!!

t_ch: 你捧起瓶身，一饮而尽……
t_en: You raise the bottle, and down it in one go...

t_ch: 秘酿淡雅的风味滑过舌尖，海面波光粼粼——
t_en: The subtle flavors of the ambrosia glide across your tongue, the sea's surface a glistening crystalline...

t_ch: …入口有非常明确的阳光、木质香、石榴果的气息……
t_en: ...Distinct sunlight, wood, pomegranate flavors on the palate...

t_ch: …这风味让你陷入幻想，画面逐渐浮现——你是一颗挂在枝头的石榴果，智慧花园惬意的暖风吹拂，心情也变得愉悦……
t_en: ...This flavor draws you into a dreamy fantasy, its visuals gradually emerging — you are a pomegranate hanging from a branch, basking 'neath the warm breeze in the Garden of Wisdom, your mood uplifted...

t_ch: …等等！口中的回味…怎么变得…像…像…洗澡水？？？
t_en: ...Hang on! This aftertaste in my mouth... Why does it taste like... like... bathwater???

t_ch: 谁会用石榴泡澡啊——！！！
t_en: Who takes baths in pomegranate juice!!!

t_ch: 你捧起瓶身，一饮而尽……
t_en: You raise the bottle, and down it in one go...

t_ch: 秘酿淡雅的风味滑过舌尖，海面波光粼粼——
t_en: The subtle flavors of the ambrosia glide across your tongue, the sea's surface a glistening crystalline...

t_ch: 适度的沉醉让你变得健谈。难道是瑟希斯附体？你变得博学，你的风趣又逗得众人大笑，不知不觉你的身边围满了青睐有加的听众。
t_en: An appropriate level of inebriation makes you chattier. Could you be possessed by Cerces? You become erudite, your witticisms tickle the crowd's roaring fancies, and before you know it, you're surrounded by your avid, admiring listeners.

t_ch: 一位生命花园的园丁被你的「球棒动力学理论」深深折服，盛情邀请你加入奥赫玛权威的学术组织。可你深知一切仅是享乐女神的愚弄，你的智慧会在系统时午夜<unbreak>12</unbreak>点后消失……
t_en: A Garden of Life Gardener is deeply engrossed in your "Baseball Bat Dynamics Theory," and heartily invites you to join a prestigious academic organization in Okhema. But you are deeply aware that this is but a trick by the goddess of pleasure — your intellect will fade when the system time strikes midnight...

t_ch: 你只好匆匆逃离浴场，留下一只鞋子供人思念……
t_en: You have no choice but to flee the bath in a hurry, leaving behind a shoe for others to remember you by...

t_ch: 你捧起瓶身，一饮而尽……
t_en: You raise the bottle, and down it in one go...

t_ch: 秘酿浓郁的风味连通全身的感官，渔船开始颠簸，风雨欲来——
t_en: The ambrosia's thick flavor surges through all your senses, and the fishing boat starts to sway, heralding the approach of a storm...

t_ch: …你脑中浮出一位故人的身影……
t_en: ...An acquaintance's figure appears in your mind...

t_ch: …即使不太理智，你还是决定拨出那串号码……
t_en: ...Even if somewhat irrational, you decide to dial that string of numbers...

t_ch: …等待的漫长像不见月光的黑夜，熟悉的声音才从听筒传来……
t_en: ...The prolonged wait feels like a night with no moonlight in sight, and a familiar voice comes through the receiver...

t_ch: 「…对不起，您不在星际通讯服务区内，请稍后再拨……」
t_en: "...Sorry, you are not within interstellar communications service range. Please try again later..."

t_ch: 你捧起瓶身，一饮而尽……
t_en: You raise the bottle, and down it in one go...

t_ch: 秘酿浓郁的风味连通全身的感官，渔船开始颠簸，风雨欲来——
t_en: The ambrosia's thick flavor surges through all your senses, and the fishing boat starts to sway, heralding the approach of a storm...

t_ch: 你不敢相信眼前所见，你再一次用力揉了揉眼……
t_en: You can hardly believe what you're seeing, and you rub your eyes hard once more...

t_ch: 眼前站着熟悉的蓝色的…红色的…绿色眼睛的朋友，只是他巧嘴滑舌的功夫不知被谁夺了去，人也变得缄默……
t_en: Before you stands a familiar blue... red... green-eyed friend. His usually glib verbosity has been somehow stolen, his person irrevocably mute...

t_ch: 他为何在此，你说不清。他邀请你骑上宽厚的背，你们游遍了奥赫玛的每个角落，你们笑语欢声，度过了难忘的一天……
t_en: Why he is here, you cannot say. He invites you to ride atop his broad back, and you journey to every corner of Okhema, spending an unforgettable day peppered with laughter...

t_ch: 这位客人，下次别拽大地兽进浴场了。
t_en: Hello, please do not shove a dromas into the bath.

t_ch: 你捧起瓶身，一饮而尽……
t_en: You raise the bottle, and down it in one go...

t_ch: 你捧起瓶身，一饮而尽。秘酿强烈的风味如一场风暴，连泰坦也会被放倒——
t_en: You raise the bottle and down it into your hatch. The ambrosia's intense flavor hits you like a storm, capable of toppling even a Titan...

t_ch: 一阵天旋地转后，你在熟悉的地方醒来……
t_en: After a wave of vertigo, you wake up in a familiar place...

t_ch: …你感受到室友的目光——他对你的所为只字不言，而根据你对他多年的了解，那目光精准包含了三分担心…五分鄙夷…十分嫌弃。
t_en: ...You feel your roommate's stare — he says nothing of your actions, but having known him for so many years, that stare accurately comprises three parts worry... five parts disdain... and a chock-full of loathing.

t_ch: 下次再这样乱来，我就不捞你了。
t_en: If you get like this again, I won't be here to pick you up.

t_ch: 当面对峙！
t_en: Confrontation time!

t_ch: 你誓死捍卫自己在浴场畅饮秘酿的权利，单方面与丹恒争得面红耳赤……
t_en: You swear to defend — to the death — your right to drink ambrosia in the bath, and engage in a one-sided argument with Dan Heng till you're flushed and furious...
Sigh...

t_ch: 胡言乱语的你被丹恒请出了房间。
t_en: Dan Heng ejects your yammering self from the room.

t_ch: 委屈哭泣…
t_en: Wail like you're wronged...

t_ch: 丹恒话语中的有所保留，无疑留给你巨大的想象的空间……
t_en: Dan Heng's words are measured, indubitably leaving enormous room for your imagination to go into overdrive...

t_ch: 你在脑中发散着可能出过的洋相，面部表情变化莫测得像所有阿哈面具的叠加体。最终你抹着泪逃离了房间……
t_en: Your mind maps out possible scenarios where you could've made a fool of yourself, your facial expressions contorting in ways that can only be described as all of Aha's masks stacked on top of one another. Finally, you wipe your tears and flee the room...

t_ch: 喂！
t_en: Hey!

t_ch: 你捧起瓶身，一饮而尽……
t_en: You raise the bottle, and down it in one go...

t_ch: 你捧起瓶身，一饮而尽。秘酿强烈的风味如一场风暴，连泰坦也会被放倒——
t_en: You raise the bottle and down it into your hatch. The ambrosia's intense flavor hits you like a storm, capable of toppling even a Titan...

t_ch: 如沉睡的泰坦一样伟大，你在世界之巅醒来，圣城不过一道俯瞰的风景……
t_en: As grand as a slumbering Titan, you awaken at the peak of the world, the holy city nothing more than a speck of landscape in the distance...

t_ch: 与如何登上高台同样费解的，是如何回到地面，四下并无楼梯、电梯或传送锚点，你选择——
t_en: As to how you inexplicably scaled to such heights, the same question arises with your descent. There are no staircases, elevators, or teleport anchors about. You choose to...

t_ch: 我占山为王！
t_en: I'm king of this mountain now!

t_ch: …一突然窜出的男子打破了你的美梦。他介绍此处为奥赫玛国家一级景区，自己是背你爬上来的导游，需要你交付<unbreak>5000</unbreak>利衡币的门票钱和<unbreak>20000</unbreak>利衡币的辛苦费。
t_en: ...A man suddenly pops up, disrupting your reverie. He introduces this place as Okhema's top national tourist attraction, and that he was the guide who carried you up the mountain. He also requests that you pay <unbreak>5,000</unbreak> Balance Coins in admission fee and <unbreak>20,000</unbreak> Balance Coins for the "service rendered."

t_ch: 你优秀的防诈骗意识让你起了疑心，在对方的纠缠不休下，你打开地图选择锚点传送走了——
t_en: Your excellent mental scam radar alerts you, and amidst the other party's persistence, you open up your map, choose an Anchor and teleport away...

t_ch: 呼叫星穹列车。
t_en: Call the Astral Express.

t_ch: 你打电话求助丹恒——
t_en: You ring up Dan Heng for help...

t_ch: 聪明的人总有别样的魅力，他神乎其技地偷来了你所在高台的建筑图纸，找到了下行的密道……如果没有九千九百九十九级台阶就更好了。
t_en: Smart people often have unique charms — he has adroitly stolen the building blueprint of the elevated platform you're on, and found the underground secret passage... Now if only there weren't nine-thousand, nine-hundred and ninety-nine steps to walk down.

t_ch: 你捧起瓶身，一饮而尽……
t_en: You raise the bottle, and down it in one go...

t_ch: 入口的秘酿消散得悄无痕迹，如吞入迷雾，你的思绪飘至另一个空间——
t_en: The ambrosia dissipates without a trace on the palate, like swallowing a mist. Your thoughts drift into another space...

t_ch: 空间中只有迷雾，不时传来水波与木头碰撞的声音，咿咿呀呀。尽头有盏昏暗的灯，你的小船向它驶去……
t_en: There is only fog in the space, with the occasional sounds of water lapping against wood, softly creaking. A dim lamp barely lights the end of the passage, your little boat drawn towards it...

t_ch: 光源飘来了甜美的歌声，让人忆起海风的自然之声，又带有人性的逗弄与撩拨，随它飘荡或缓缓地掩沉。你选择——
t_en: Sweet song trails into the light source, reminiscent of nature's sea breeze, bringing with it the laughter and allure of humans, drifting with it or slowly sinking. You choose to...

t_ch: 堵住耳朵。
t_en: Stuff your ears.

t_ch: 在海上…不见人形…悦耳歌声…一串关键词触发了你的防御机制，你掏出口袋里的棉花团塞住了耳朵——
t_en: On the sea... No one around... Pleasing song... A string of keywords triggers your defense mechanism. You take out cotton balls from your pocket and stuff your ears...

t_ch: 帕姆玩偶填充物质量果真不错，耳旁的声音变得渺远……
t_en: The quality of this Pom-Pom doll stuffing is pretty good — the sounds you're hearing start to fade away...

t_ch: 远处的灯光熄灭了……
t_en: The distant light has gone out...

t_ch: 用魔法打败魔法。
t_en: Defeat magic with magic.

t_ch: 你回忆起银河著名女歌手的教诲，扯着嗓子以歌声回应。
t_en: You recall the teachings of a songstress famed across the cosmos, responding to the song at the top of your voice.

t_ch: 或许是节拍不准，或许是音色不悦，声音的主人似乎拒绝与你合唱。见你没有停下的意思，骂骂咧咧地潜入了海中。
t_en: Perhaps the beat is wrong, or the tone isn't pleasant enough. The owner of the voice refuses your request for a duet. Seeing that you have no intention of stopping, the owner irately dives into the sea, spouting profanities in a trail of foamy bubbles.

t_ch: 远处的灯光熄灭了……
t_en: The distant light has gone out...

t_ch: 靠近它。
t_en: Get close to it.

t_ch: 你离歌声越来越近，灯火触手可及……
t_en: You get closer to the voice, the lamp within arm's reach...

t_ch: 一条从海底跃起的鲸鱼，身姿优雅而惊人，它一口吞下那盏灯光，带着它沉入海底，而后拍起巨浪，将你的小船推向远方……
t_en: A whale leaps from the ocean depths, its movements elegant yet astonishing, swallowing that lamp with a gulp, and taking it back to the sunken deep. It ends its routine with a giant splash, slapping your small boat farther into the distance...

t_ch: …你失去了意识……
t_en: ...You lose consciousness...

t_ch: 一段从未听闻的跫音…今日竟果真有幸相逢。你好，奥赫玛的贵客。
t_en: Sounds of footfalls never before heard... I didn't think I'd actually get to encounter you today. Greetings, honored guest of Okhema.

t_ch: 怎会如此，几时暴露的！
t_en: Wuh, I've been made!?

t_ch: 放轻松就好，{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}。
t_en: Just relax, {F#Miss}{M#Mister} {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你并没有暴露身份，阿格莱雅女士也不会找你的麻烦。
t_en: You did not reveal yourself, and neither will Lady Aglaea cause trouble for you.

t_ch: 非也，我来自塔拉，塔拉萨……
t_en: I'm not a guest, I am from Th-Thalassa...

t_ch: 塔拉萨波利斯，对吗？你和另一位朋友用这个杜撰的地名乔装成异邦人。
t_en: Thalassa's Thalassopolis, is that right? You and your friend used this fake location name and pretended to be travelers.

t_ch: 不必惊慌，我并无揭穿你们的打算。
t_en: Do not panic. I have no intention of exposing you.

t_ch: 我们在哪见过吗？
t_en: I'm sorry, do we know each other?

t_ch: 呵…若我说，我曾在梦中与你相见，你愿意相信么，{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}？
t_en: Hah... If I said that we met in a dream once, would you believe me, {F#Miss}{M#Mister} {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 我名为帕西提亚，是{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}的祭司。换言之，我曾是雅努斯的圣女。
t_en: I am Pasithea, priestess of {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}. In other words, I was once the Holy Maiden of Janus.

t_ch: 自「黄金战争」至今的漫长岁月里，我一直担任着传达欧洛尼斯神谕的职责。
t_en: In the long while between the Chrysos War and now, I have been tasked with delivering Oronyx's prophecies.

t_ch: 我来到奥赫玛也有些时日了。如今我不再受传谕职责所囿，可以用预言替居民们解决生活上的小麻烦。
t_en: I have arrived in Okhema for some time now. I am not bound by the duty to deliver prophecies any longer, so I use them to help citizens resolve what little troubles they encounter in daily life.

t_ch: 听起来和缇宝的工作好像！
t_en: Sounds just like Tribbie's job!

t_ch: 缇里西庇俄丝……
t_en: Tribios...

t_ch: 帕西提亚的脸上似乎蒙了些阴霾。她垂着头，轻吁了一口气。
t_en: Pasithea's face appears to be clouded by a gray haze. Her head is bowed, and she sighs lightly.

t_ch: 抱歉，{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}，我与缇宝大人或许算有些…纠葛，可以的话，我不愿谈论这个话题。
t_en: Apologies, {F#Miss}{M#Mister} {NICKNAME}, there are some... complications between me and Lady Tribbie. I would prefer not to discuss this topic, if possible.

t_ch: 希望您不要误会，缇宝大人是个很好的人。只是…我与她们至今仍不知道该如何面对彼此。
t_en: I hope you won't misunderstand, for Lady Tribbie is a very kind person. It is just... I don't know how to face them, even after all this time.

t_ch: 帕西提亚重新抬起头，像是经过一番心理建设，她的神情重新变得恬静温柔。
t_en: Pasithea raises her head, as if she has just prepared herself mentally for something. Her expression once again becomes calm and gentle.

t_ch: 我…有些失态，还请{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}见谅。
t_en: I... was a little out of line. Please forgive me, {F#Miss}{M#Mister} {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 话归正题吧。今日相逢，亦可说是命运的安排，我特意提前推去了其它居民的邀约。若阁下有兴趣，我们可以再多聊几句。
t_en: Let's get back on topic. Our meeting today could be fate's design. I even specifically declined invitations from the other residents beforehand. We can chat a bit more if you are interested.

t_ch: 一段略感熟悉的跫音…今日竟有幸再度相逢，你好，{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}。
t_en: Sounds of footfalls somewhat familiar... We meet again, how fortunate. Hello, {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你好，帕西提亚！
t_en: Hi there, Pasithea!

t_ch: 这个姐姐我好像见过。
t_en: I think I've seen this lady before.

t_ch: 呵…看来在欧洛尼斯的夜帷已经盖住{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}与我的回忆了。
t_en: Heh... It seems Oronyx's veil of night had already covered the memories between {F#Miss}{M#Mister} {NICKNAME} and I.

t_ch: 容许我稍微掀起这夜帷的一角么？
t_en: Will you permit me to slightly lift a corner of that veil?

t_ch: 我倒要看看你怎么掀。
t_en: I wanna see how you're gonna lift it.

t_ch: 帕西提亚将指尖搭上你的耳背，天外之音轻柔地传入你的耳中。
t_en: Pasithea places her fingertips against the back of your ear, and the echo from beyond the world softly flows into your ears.

t_ch: 这段天外之音并无言语，只传达来一股温柔的情绪。闭上眼，仿若置身静谧的海面，抬首望去，阴霾与风暴已经在很远的地方了。
t_en: This echo contains no words, only passing along a gentle emotion. You close your eyes, and it feels like you are floating over a silent sea. When you look upward, the storm and clouds are already far away.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}，可有回想起什么？
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister} {NICKNAME}, do you remember anything now?

t_ch: 这下听懂了。
t_en: I understood that.

t_ch: 我全想起来了！
t_en: I remember everything now!

t_ch: 不必了！我记得你，帕西提亚。
t_en: No need! I remember you, Pasithea.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}记得我便好。
t_en: I'm glad that you remember me, {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 之前的事情…很感谢你。
t_en: Thank you... for what you did before.

t_ch: 近来睡得怎么样？
t_en: How's your sleep been lately?

t_ch: 托{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}的福，我很少再梦到那些黯淡无光的往事，也不再听见母亲的声音了。
t_en: Thanks for asking, {F#Miss}{M#Mister} {NICKNAME}. I've rarely dreamed of those dark memories of the past, and I don't hear my mother's voice anymore.

t_ch: 最开始确实很不适应，第二天醒来总空落落的。但是不久以后，心绪就安宁下来了。我想母亲也会替我高兴吧。
t_en: It was hard to get used to at first. I would feel so empty when I woke up in the mornings. But my mind started to feel calm after a while. I imagine my mother would be happy to know that.

t_ch: 还会做奇怪的梦么？
t_en: Do you still have strange dreams?

t_ch: 托{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}的福，我很少再梦到那些黯淡无光的往事，也不再听见母亲的声音了。
t_en: Thanks for asking, {F#Miss}{M#Mister} {NICKNAME}. I've rarely dreamed of those dark memories of the past, and I don't hear my mother's voice anymore.

t_ch: 至于奇怪的梦…如果我说，见到阁下的前一晚总会梦到阁下，你愿意相信么？
t_en: As for strange dreams... If I tell you that I always dream of you the night before we see each other, would you believe me?

t_ch: 话归正题吧。今日重逢，亦可说是命运的安排，我特意提前推去了其它居民的邀约。若阁下有兴趣，我们可以再多聊几句。
t_en: Let's get back on topic. Our reunion today could be fate's design. I even specifically declined invitations from the other residents beforehand. We can chat a bit more if you are interested.

t_ch: 啊…一段略感熟悉的跫音。{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}，有幸与你再度重逢。
t_en: Ahh... A rather familiar footstep. {F#Miss}{M#Mister} {NICKNAME}, it's a pleasure to see you again.

t_ch: 难得再会，要多聊几句吗？
t_en: It's such a rare occasion to run into you again. Would you like to chat?

t_ch: 我想跟你聊聊…
t_en: I'd like to chat with you...

t_ch: 当然可以，想聊些什么？关于你和我，还是过去和未来？
t_en: Of course, what do you want to talk about? About you and me? Or about the past and the future?

t_ch: 给我算算命！
t_en: Tell me my fortune!

t_ch: 呵…有些任性的要求。记得不要说出去——欧洛尼斯的力量，本不该这么使用的。
t_en: Hah... A somewhat brazen request. Remember not to tell anyone else — Oronyx's power is not meant to be used like this.

t_ch: 帕西提亚将指尖搭上你的耳背，天外之音轻柔地传入你的耳中。
t_en: Pasithea places her fingertips against the back of your ear, and the echo from beyond the world softly flows into your ears.

t_ch: 你听见一阵轻柔的天外之声，像是有人摇动了用花瓣与雨滴做成的风铃。
t_en: You hear a gentle, celestial sound, like someone shaking chimes made of petals and raindrops.

t_ch: 你的心绪变得更加平静了……
t_en: Your mind has become even calmer...

t_ch: 你是个幸运的孩子。幸运，亦即安宁。或许你会觉得今天有些无聊，但是……
t_en: You are a lucky child. Luck denotes peace. Perhaps you may find today humdrum, but...

t_ch: 能够享受这份足够顺遂的「无聊」，不也是一种幸福吗？
t_en: To be able to enjoy this fortuitous "humdrum." Is that not a form of joy as well?

t_ch: 你听见一阵澎湃的天外之声，像是一朵鼓着腮帮子的云吹响了管乐器。
t_en: You hear a roaring celestial sound, like a cloud with puffed cheeks billowing into a wind instrument.

t_ch: 你不禁勾起了嘴角……
t_en: You instinctively curl the corners of your lips...

t_ch: 看哪，机遇在大踏步地朝你走来…当然，我看不到，但我听到了它稳稳当当的脚步声。
t_en: Look, opportunity is striding towards you... Of course, I can't see, but I can hear its steady footsteps.

t_ch: 请你也用稳稳当当的心境去迎接它吧。
t_en: Please meet it with a steady state of mind.

t_ch: 你听见一阵可爱的天外之声，像是小型动物打着呼噜，在窸窸窣窣进食。
t_en: You hear a wave of adorable celestial sounds, like small animals snoring and rustling through their food.

t_ch: 你心中柔软的部分被有所触动……
t_en: The soft parts in your heart have been touched...

t_ch: 你最近一定遇见了有趣的人、事、物。多么好呀！听见这样充满生机的声音，我也想稍微跳跳舞了。
t_en: You must have run into an interesting person, event, or thing recently. How pleasant! So full of life. I might break into a little dance as well.

t_ch: …开玩笑啦，我从没跳过舞。而你呢，一定不要浪费这样好的心境啊。
t_en: ...I jest. Never danced in my life. And you, you must not squander such a wonderful inner state.

t_ch: 你听见一阵幽婉的天外之声，像是有谁在雨天悄悄地踩着水潭渐行渐远。
t_en: You hear a profound celestial sound, like someone quietly pitter-pattering along a stream into the distance on a rainy day.

t_ch: 你不知为何有些鼻酸……
t_en: You don't know why this stings your nose with subtle sorrow...

t_ch: …朋友，告别向来尤为艰难。
t_en: ...Friend, saying goodbye is always hard.

t_ch: 如果真的发生了遗憾的事情，愿欧洛尼斯给你一个美好的梦境。
t_en: If something sad has truly happened, may Oronyx gift you with a beautiful dreamscape.

t_ch: 你听见一阵低沉的天外之声，像是本无生命之物用尽力气发出的叹息。
t_en: You hear a low celestial sound, like something inherently lifeless sighing with every fiber of its shell.

t_ch: 你感觉有些疲惫……
t_en: You feel a little fatigued...

t_ch: 或许近来你有些疲倦。别太勉强自己，就算像山之民那般坚韧如岩石，也会有累坏的时候。
t_en: Perhaps you've been tired out recently. Don't push yourself too hard. Even a sturdy rock as tenacious as a Mountain Dweller will be burned out sometimes.

t_ch: 要是觉得累了，就去浴场泡泡澡吧。
t_en: If you feel lethargic, you should visit the baths.

t_ch: 你听见一阵细微的天外之声，像是一枚绚丽的泡泡慢悠悠地朝天上飞去。
t_en: You hear a wave of faint celestial sounds, like a bubble floating leisurely into the sky.

t_ch: 你不禁祈祷——别破，别破，别破……
t_en: You can't help but pray — don't break, don't break, don't break...

t_ch: 我的耳廓里盈满了幸福的声音。但它们就要溜走了。
t_en: My ears are filled to the brim with sounds of joy. But they are about to slip away.

t_ch: 假如一枚泡泡快要破碎，不妨再吹一枚？
t_en: If a bubble is about to pop, why not blow another?

t_ch: 请不要太过当真，我只是一位祭司，领受的欧洛尼斯赐福终归是有限的。
t_en: Please do not take this too seriously. I am only a priest — there are limits, even if I have received Oronyx's blessing.

t_ch: 而且{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}教会了我…只要愿意做出改变，即便无情如命运，或许也会绽出崭新的生机。
t_en: And {F#Miss}{M#Mister} {NICKNAME} has taught me... As long as one is willing to make a change, even destiny, merciless as it is, may blossom with new life.

t_ch: 现在你愿意聊聊缇宝了吗？
t_en: Are you ready to talk about Tribbie now?

t_ch: 重新回顾了那段记忆后，我似乎有些…理解缇宝大人了。
t_en: After reminiscing on that memory, I somewhat... understand Lady Tribbie.

t_ch: 同为雅努萨波利斯的圣女，缇宝大人做出了与我相反的选择。
t_en: As a Holy Maiden of Janusopolis, Lady Tribbie made a choice contrary to mine.

t_ch: 我不知道她经历了些什么，但我猜…那肯定也是一段叫人咬紧牙关、说不出话的沉重往事吧。
t_en: I don't know what she went through, but I'm guessing... it must have been a heavy past that renders one's teeth clenched and one's mouth speechless.

t_ch: 你现在还会避着她们吗？
t_en: Are you still avoiding them now?

t_ch: 嗯…我扪心自问，还是放不下对缇宝大人的复杂心绪。也许今后会有机会与她们敞开心扉说说话……
t_en: Yes... If I were honest with myself, I still can't relinquish my complicated feelings towards Lady Tribbie. Maybe after today, there might come a chance to talk with them about what happened...

t_ch: 但至少现在的我，还做不到。
t_en: But for now, I am unable to do so.

t_ch: 想听你聊聊你的家乡。
t_en: I want to hear about your hometown.

t_ch: 雅努萨波利斯…是我的家乡，仅此而已。
t_en: Janusopolis... is my home. That is all.

t_ch: 我曾听说过雅努萨波利斯鼎盛期的辉煌往事。作为「奥赫玛同盟」的重要议事场所，雅努萨波利斯曾是一座富裕安宁的城邦。
t_en: I once heard of Janusopolis' glorious past during her height of power. As an essential Council venue within the Okhema Alliance, Janusopolis was once a prosperous and peaceful city.

t_ch: 每次求取神谕，命运三相殿外都会大摆筵席，就连平民也可以在外围参与，遥望圣女的庄严身姿。
t_en: Each time a prophecy was sought, the Temple of the Three Fates would host a grand banquet, where even civilians could gaze their eyes upon the solemn figure of the Holy Maiden from the peripheries.

t_ch: 可那与我又有何干？我出生时「黄金战争」已经打响，乱世之中，再伟大的城邦都免不了衰落，雅努萨波利斯的平民不得不在饥荒中嚼土充饥……
t_en: But I could not experience any of that. When I was born, the Chrysos War had already begun. In times of chaos, even the grandest cities cannot escape decline. Janusopolis' people had to eat dirt to stave off hunger during the famine...

t_ch: 而如今…它已经彻底死去，成为一处阴冷的重渊。
t_en: And now... she is completely gone, turned into a cold, unfathomable abyss.

t_ch: 重温过往的天外之音。
t_en: Revisit the celestial echoes of the past.

t_ch: 没想到，你竟如此挂念那城邦的往事…那就让欧洛尼斯来为你重演那逝去的岁月吧。
t_en: I never expected you to be so nostalgic for that city's past... Then let Oronyx reenact for you those years gone by.

t_ch: 那么，你想听闻哪段天外之音呢？
t_en: So, what kind of celestial echo would you like to listen to?

t_ch: 聆听祭官的彷徨。
t_en: Listen to the priest's dithering.

t_ch: 这天外之音来自那侍奉泰坦之人们的祷告，然而他们的祷告从来不是为了取悦神明，而是为了令神明庇护自己…最虔诚的信徒，或许就是最胆怯的懦夫……
t_en: This celestial echo comes from the prayers of those serving the Titans. But their prayers were never to entertain the gods, but to have them protect the worshipers... The most devout of disciples, and perhaps also the most craven of all...

t_ch: 聆听市民的蠢动。
t_en: Listen to the civilians' plights.

t_ch: 这天外之音来自雅努萨波利斯的动荡，人们享受着公正的庇护、门径的指引、天帷的启示…而当天秤失衡、门径闭锁、天帷隐没之时，那脆弱的希望便会化作绝望……
t_en: This celestial echo comes from the panic that spread across Janusopolis. People enjoyed the protection of justice, the guidance of the passage, the revelations of the veil... Yet the day the scale was tipped, the passage was sealed, and the veil went missing, those frail hopes were ground into cries of despair...

t_ch: 聆听灾难的逼近。
t_en: Listen to the encroaching disaster.

t_ch: 这天外之音来自欧洛尼斯映照出的可怖景色，纷争掀翻大地，天帷遮蔽天空，一切沦于死亡。这命运一次又一次在翁法罗斯重演，受命运守护的城邦也难幸免……
t_en: This celestial echo comes from the horrific scene reflected by Oronyx. Strife split the earth, the veil blanketed the sky, and all floundered in death. This fate has been played out in Amphoreus time and time again — even the city protected by destiny itself cannot escape misfortune...

t_ch: 聆听厄境的惨状。
t_en: Listen to the horrors of adversity.

t_ch: 这天外之音来自雅努萨波利斯覆灭的哀嚎，黑潮如洪水袭来，将那荣光显耀的圣殿冲垮，从中滋生的魔物撕碎了三位泰坦的眷顾，让恐惧吞没了这座城，直到……
t_en: This celestial echo comes from the wails of Janusopolis' razing. The black tide assails the city in a deluge, crashing through that glorious, sacred temple, the monsters spawning from its midst tearing through the three Titans' kin, sowing horror that devoured this city, until...

t_ch: 聆听你那时的心声。
t_en: Listen to your heart's voice during that time.

t_ch: 这天外之音来自一个被称为圣女的骗子，一个目不能视却要指引他人的盲者，名为帕西提亚的凡俗女子的心……
t_en: This celestial echo comes from a liar conferred with the title of Holy Maiden. A blind person who couldn't see guiding the masses, the heart of an ordinary woman named Pasithea...

t_ch: 呵呵，向他人展示心境还真是有些难为情…但若是你，我倒并不介意。
t_en: Hehe, it's a bit embarrassing baring my emotions for others to see like this... But I don't actually mind at all if it's you.

t_ch: 再听听其他的天外之音。
t_en: Listen to the other celestial echoes.

t_ch: 结束吧。
t_en: Let's finish up.

t_ch: 今天有些忙，就聊到这吧。
t_en: I'm busy today, so let's put a pin in things there.

t_ch: 无妨，{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}。白日漫漫、长夜无垠，我们还会再见的。
t_en: It's okay, {F#Miss}{M#Mister} {NICKNAME}. The days are long, the nights are endless, and we'll see each other again.

t_ch: 今天有些忙，就聊到这吧。
t_en: I'm busy today, so let's put a pin in things there.

t_ch: 无妨，{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}。白日漫漫、长夜无垠，我们还会再见的。
t_en: It's okay, {F#Miss}{M#Mister} {NICKNAME}. The days are long, the nights are endless, and we'll see each other again.

t_ch: 你好，「异乡人」阁下。上次那张悬锋城的照片，有帮上你们的忙吗？
t_en: Hello, esteemed outlander. Was that photo of Castrum Kremnos helpful to you?

t_ch: 太有帮助了！
t_en: Immensely helpful!

t_ch: 那就好。我始终对你有些愧疚，要是能帮上忙，我的心里也会好受些。
t_en: That's good to hear. I've always felt somewhat guilty toward you, so knowing I've been able to help eases my mind.

t_ch: 还好吧，不是很重要。
t_en: It's not a big deal.

t_ch: 这样…我始终对你有些愧疚，要是有什么需要帮忙的地方，请尽管来找我。
t_en: In that case, I've always felt a certain guilt toward you. If there's anything you need help with, please don't hesitate to ask.

t_ch: 虽然阿格莱雅大人禁止我继续探索「天外之界」，但我对世界的好奇心不会停止。要是有兴趣的话，可以随时来找我聊天。
t_en: Though Lady Aglaea has forbidden me from further exploring the world beyond the sky, my curiosity remains unabated. If you're interested, feel free to chat with me anytime.

t_ch: 我很乐意与阁下分享我的最新发现。
t_en: I'd be delighted to share my latest discoveries with you, esteemed outlander.

t_ch: 啊，「异乡人」阁下。来得正好，我达米亚诺斯最近又有了伟大的发现。
t_en: Ah, esteemed outlander. Your timing is impeccable. I, Damionis, have recently made yet another significant discovery.

t_ch: 我在刻法勒的脚下从门扉时蹲守到幕匿时，滴水未进，也没将视线移开过哪怕分毫，终于…终于让我发现了「黎明机器」的秘密！
t_en: I've been stationed at the base of Kephale from Entry Hour to Curtain-Fall Hour, fasting and unblinking, until at last... I've unraveled the mysteries of the Dawn Device!

t_ch: 那就是——「黎明机器」的光亮并非一成不变，使用最高精度的透镜观察时，可以看到极其微弱的明度变化，虽然不明显，但确实存在！
t_en: That is — the light emitted by the Dawn Device is not constant. When observed through the highest precision lenses, one can detect extremely subtle fluctuations in brightness. Although these changes are not readily apparent, they are indeed real and measurable.

t_ch: 这说明，传统的计时方式，根本不适合现在的奥赫玛！
t_en: This indicates that the traditional method of timekeeping is utterly unsuitable for modern Okhema!

t_ch: 你不是个探险家嘛？
t_en: Aren't you an explorer?

t_ch: 探索奥赫玛里不为人知的秘密，这难道不是令人兴奋的冒险吗？
t_en: Isn't delving into the unknown secrets of Okhema an exhilarating adventure?

t_ch: 好吧，其实是因为我曾在阿格莱雅大人的面前立过誓约，不再去尝试探索「天外之界」，至少这段时间里得装装样子。
t_en: Well, actually, I had sworn in front of Lady Aglaea not to try to explore the world beyond the sky. I need to keep up appearances, at least for the time being.

t_ch: 真是伟大的发现啊！
t_en: What a great discovery!

t_ch: 对吧！要是将这个发现公之于众，我们就能改变奥赫玛沿用至今的历法了！
t_en: Exactly! By sharing this discovery, we could revolutionize the longstanding calendar system in Okhema!

t_ch: 想想看，远古时期就得到广泛认可，据说是由刻法勒创立的五大时段，竟然被证实需要修改，这是多么令人震惊的发现啊！
t_en: Imagine this — the five periods of the day, established by Kephale in ancient times and widely recognized ever since are now proven to need modification. What a startling discovery!

t_ch: 我得赶快把这个结果记录下来，要是不小心忘了可就糟糕了。
t_en: I should jot this down quickly — wouldn't want to let it slip my mind.

t_ch: …我没懂你的意思。
t_en: ...I don't understand what you mean.

t_ch: …这很难理解吗？来，我详细讲给你听。
t_en: ...Is this so hard to grasp? Never mind, let me explain it in detail.

t_ch: 奥赫玛会沿用传统的历法，将每天划分为五个时段，是因为人们错误地认为「黎明机器」的光芒不会改变，无法依据光照确定时间。
t_en: Okhema adheres to a traditional calendar, dividing each day into five distinct periods based on the mistaken belief that the light from the Dawn Device remains constant and cannot be used to determine time.

t_ch: 可我证明了，「黎明机器」的光线会有变化。那是不是…我们就可以依据这个来制定新的历法了？是不是我，达米亚诺斯，修正了刻法勒的创造？！
t_en: But I have proven that the light from the Dawn Device does indeed change. Could we then base a new calendar on this discovery? Am I, Damionis, actually correcting Kephale's original creation?

t_ch: 你真是太厉害了！
t_en: That's incredible!

t_ch: 谢谢，谢谢。新的发现需要探索精神，要感谢，就赞美我这自由自在、无拘无束的灵魂吧！
t_en: Thank you, thank you. The spirit of exploration deserves praise. Praise my free, unbound soul!

t_ch: 但奥赫玛不需要新的历法。
t_en: But Okhema does not need a new calendar.

t_ch: 话虽如此，我知道人们都习惯了这套计时方式，也无意接受任何改变，但……
t_en: Be that as it may, I know people are accustomed to this method of timekeeping and are not keen on any changes, but...

t_ch: 作为探险家，我还是想要去寻找未知！即便得不到实际结果，我也想要证明，我的猜想是正确的！
t_en: As an explorer, I'm driven to seek the unknown! Even if it yields no practical results, I'm determined to prove that my conjectures are correct!

t_ch: 所以你打算做些什么呢？
t_en: So what do you plan to do?

t_ch: 我会去向元老院讲述我的猜想，邀请树庭的学者们见证我的发现。我要在集市里做演讲，在浴池里举办讨论会，我要让所有人都知道这件事。
t_en: I will go to the Council of Elders with my conjectures and invite Grove scholars to witness my findings. I'll give lectures in the marketplace, and hold discussions in the baths. I want everyone to know about this.

t_ch: 分享令人激动的发现是改变的第一步——祝我成功吧，「异乡人」阁下！
t_en: Sharing exciting discoveries is the first step toward change — wish me luck, esteemed outlander!

t_ch: 拥有壮硕身躯的卫士看着你，一言不发。
t_en: A burly guard looks at you, silent as ever.

t_ch: 奇怪，按往常来说，这时候你们应该已经聊上了，要不要再试试看？
t_en: Strange, by this time you would normally have started chatting. Maybe give it another try?

t_ch: 你甚至不敢确定，在那头盔的深邃缝隙中是否存在着一双眼眸。
t_en: You can't even be sure whether a pair of eyes lurks behind that deep slit in the helmet.

t_ch: 或许，需要更多的尝试才能激起你们言语的交互。再试一次吧！
t_en: Perhaps more attempts are required to initiate a conversation. Give it another go!

t_ch: 他究竟在看你，还是在看你身后的某块云雾？
t_en: Is he truly looking at you, or is it the mist behind you that's caught his gaze?

t_ch: 你不知道，但你的热情一定打动了他——也可能没有。
t_en: You can't be certain, but surely your enthusiasm has made an impression — although it might not have.

t_ch: 直觉告诉你，治好他的社交恐惧症就差临门一脚了。再试试看吧！
t_en: Your intuition suggests you're on the verge of breaking through his social anxiety. Give it another try!

t_ch: 拜托，四次了，这个大块头怎么一点反应都没有？
t_en: Come on, this is the fourth attempt, and this big guy still hasn't reacted?

t_ch: 阿基维利在上，这沉默寡言的人你是聊定了。向他展示你「开拓」友谊的妙着吧！
t_en: Akivili above, you've marked this silent one for conversation. Now, show him what it means to forge a Trailblazing friendship!

t_ch: 我在给朋友取材，能照个相吗？
t_en: I'm gathering materials for a friend. Can I take a photo?
...Sure.

t_ch: 你拿出相机，拍了一张酷炫的艺术照，这大块头全程没动一下。
t_en: You pull out your camera and take a cool artistic shot, yet the big guy doesn't move an inch.

t_ch: 「好帅的头盔！」
t_en: "What a cool helmet!"

t_ch: …都一样。
t_en: ...They're all the same.

t_ch: 但，谢谢。
t_en: But, thank you.

t_ch: 表演倒立。
t_en: Perform a handstand.

t_ch: 你完成了一组连桂乃芬见了都要欢呼的单手倒立空翻。
t_en: You pulled off a series of one-handed handstand flips that even Guinaifen would cheer for.

t_ch: 大块头没说话，但鼓了鼓掌。
t_en: The big guy doesn't talk, but he claps.

t_ch: …异邦的勇士。
t_en: ...Warrior of the outlands.

t_ch: 你很能打。那天见过。表达感谢。
t_en: You're strong. Saw it that day. Appreciate.

t_ch: 我，执勤。再见。
t_en: I'm, on duty. Goodbye.

t_ch: 卫兵将头转回，不再理会你。随即，周遭传来了窃窃私语的声音。
t_en: The guard turns away, ignoring you completely. Shortly thereafter, whispers begin to echo around you.

t_ch: 厉害啊。我在奥赫玛呆了这么久，头一次看这位卫士理人。平常我们怎么叫他都没反应的。
t_en: Incredible. I've spent years in Okhema and this is the first time I've seen this guard acknowledge anyone. Normally, he never responds, no matter how loudly we call.

t_ch: 是啊，你瞧——哎，山之民兄弟，今天天气不错嘿？
t_en: Yeah, check this out — hey man, Mountain Dweller bro! The weather's pretty good today, isn't it?

t_ch: 他连头都没转。
t_en: He doesn't even turn his head.

t_ch: 也不知道这朋友是怎么办到的，真是稀罕事……
t_en: No one knows how that visitor did it. It is truly a rare event...

t_ch: …今天天气不错嘿？
t_en: ...Nice weather today, huh?

t_ch: …还行。
t_en: ...It's, alright.

t_ch: 你跟他们有矛盾吗？
t_en: Do you have a problem with them?

t_ch: …没有。
为什么只对我有反应？
t_en: Why do you only respond to me?

t_ch: …战士说的话，更有意义。
t_en: ...Words of warrior, more important.

t_ch: 勇士。
t_en: Warrior.

t_ch: 我想跟你聊聊天。
t_en: I'd like to chat with you.

t_ch: 我，执勤…算了，说。
t_en: I am, on duty... Never mind, speak.

t_ch: 关于你们山之民…
t_en: Regarding your people, the Mountain Dwellers...

t_ch: …对吉奥里亚的子民，好奇，你是第一个。
t_en: ...Curious about, Georios' people, you are first.

t_ch: 我们，和大地兽一起诞生。摇篮里，一般很挤。
t_en: Born with dromases. Cradle, crowded.

t_ch: 刻法勒造人，智慧和顽强，各加一半。瑟希斯造东西，只加了智慧。轮到吉奥里亚，还有剩，但不多。
t_en: Kephale gave, humans wisdom and resilience. Cerces gave, creations only wisdom. By Georios' time, little left.

t_ch: 所以，吉奥里亚加了很多土。让我们，又高又壮。用双臂，代替言语。
t_en: Georios gave us, lot of earth, making us tall and strong. We talk with, our arms.

t_ch: 但还是，被嘲笑。没有工作适合。有的，当了铁匠。有的，当了铁块。赞美刻法勒，赞美塔兰顿。我们，当了卫士。
t_en: Still, we are mocked. No good jobs. Some became, blacksmiths. Others just iron blocks. We praise Kephale, praise Talanton. We are now guards.

t_ch: 另外，羡慕大地兽。它们有很多智慧。至少，比我们多。
t_en: We envy dromases. They have wisdom. At least, more than us.

t_ch: 讲讲你的故事！
t_en: Tell me a story about you!

t_ch: …没有故事，只有真事。
t_en: ...Only truths, no stories.

t_ch: 奥赫玛的卫士，基本都是山之民。我们，敬业。
t_en: Most guards, in Okhema, Mountain Dwellers. We dedicated.

t_ch: 也有，怪胎。揍一顿，会好些。
t_en: Some odd. Beating them helps.

t_ch: 雅努斯，不好。密径、传信石版，都太小，吉奥里亚的子民难用。
t_en: Janus unsuitable. Paths, teleslates too small, for Georios' kin.

t_ch: 一个月，会弄坏一次。好在，不用赔。听说，会有我们专用的。不知道，要多少光历年。
t_en: Breaks once a month. No repayment required. Heard specialized ones, being made for us. Not known, how many Light Calendar years needed.

t_ch: 塔兰顿的神殿，这里没有。有祭司，一半难找，一半渎职。
t_en: Temple of Talanton, don't exist here. Priests, hard to find, or corrupt.

t_ch: 所以卫士，会向彼此行礼。因为，每个卫士，都很塔兰顿。
t_en: Guards, salute each other. Each embodies, Talanton deeply.

t_ch: 我的房子，很重。背到城外，很累。为了更好执勤，值得。
t_en: My house, heavy. Moving outside city, tiring. Necessary for, better duty.

t_ch: 但是，是土做的。要防大地兽。
t_en: House made of earth. Must protect from dromases.

t_ch: 大地兽，舌头很硬。被舔，很难受。听说，那代表，它们喜欢你。
t_en: Dromases' tongues, hard. Licking discomforts. I heard, it signifies, their affection.

t_ch: 希望它们，离远些。真的很难受。
t_en: Prefer dromases, to stay distant. Very uncomfortable.

t_ch: 据说，吉奥里亚的子民，不会见到塞纳托斯，只会回归大地。
t_en: Georios' kin, do not meet Thanatos. Simply return to the earth.

t_ch: 很遗憾，本来还想，和老朋友打个招呼。
t_en: Too bad, I wanted to greet, old friend.

t_ch: 没事，守城辛苦！
t_en: No worries, guarding the city is tough work!

t_ch: 嗯。
t_en: Yeah.

t_ch: 没事，守城辛苦！
t_en: No worries, guarding the city is tough work!

t_ch: 嗯。
t_en: Yeah.

t_ch: 亲爱的帕门弥德。请告诉我：你是谁，你从哪里来，向哪里去？
t_en: My dear Parmenides. Please, tell me: Who are you? Where have you come from? Where are you going?

t_ch: 我是阿卡索斯的帕门弥德，对大地的信仰如同吉奥里亚的神躯般坚定。我从云石天宫来，洗净我的身体与思想后，正要往城门口去。
t_en: I am Parmenides of Icatus. My faith in the earth is as firm as the divine body of Georios. I have come from Marmoreal Palace. Now that I have washed my mind and body, I shall head for the city gates.

t_ch: 很好，你的回答极其详尽。那么我又要发问了：你所述说的阿卡索斯城，与我所知的那座城邦是否为同一物？请别急着反驳，仔细思考我的提问。
t_en: Very good. Such detailed answers. Then allow me to ask you one more thing: This city of Icatus that you speak of... Is it the same as the city that I know of? Do not rush to refute me. Think carefully about what it is that I am asking.

t_ch: 奥赫玛识字的公民都知道，阿卡索斯早已在黑潮中覆灭。你如何从一座不存在的城邦来？
t_en: As all who can read in Okhema know that Icatus was long since destroyed by the black tide. How could you have come from a city that does not exist?

t_ch: （继续听下去）
t_en: (Keep listening)

t_ch: 我对这种辩论没有兴趣。
t_en: I have no interest in this kind of debates.

t_ch: 是啊，翁法罗斯还有那么多有趣的事物等着你去开拓，为什么非得花时间来思考这些无用的内容呢。你不认为这是个有价值的话题。
t_en: You're right. Amphoreus has many interesting things awaiting trailblazing. Why must you waste time reflecting on such pointless conversations? This topic is clearly of no value to you.

t_ch: 是我刚才的表述有误。我曾来自阿卡索斯，那是我的故乡，黑潮来临前，我携带所有财产逃来了奥赫玛。我来自尚未覆灭的阿卡索斯，这点千真万确。
t_en: I made a mistake in my last remark. I originally came from Icatus, and that is my home. Before the black tide descended, I took all of my worldly belongings and fled to Okhema. I come from an Icatus that has not yet been destroyed. This point is absolutely true.

t_ch: 覆灭前的阿卡索斯，与现今被黑潮吞没的阿卡索斯，是否为同一物？
t_en: Are the Icatus that existed before its destruction and the Icatus that has now been devoured by the black tide the same thing?

t_ch: 当然。无论何时，那里都是我的家乡。
t_en: Of course. No matter what time we speak of, Icatus is my hometown.

t_ch: 覆灭前的阿卡索斯停留在你的记忆中，被黑潮吞没的阿卡索斯却可以随时前往。早已不存在的东西，如何与现今仍存在的东西是同一物？
t_en: The Icatus that existed before its destruction remains in your memory, yet you can go to the Icatus that has been devoured by the black tide at any time. How could something long since gone be the same as something that exists currently?

t_ch: 眼睛看不见，手摸不着，难道就是不存在？若是如此，那世间将无物存在，因为所有事物都处在流变当中，我们也无法谈论任何内容。
t_en: Are you saying that if I can neither see it with my eyes nor feel it with my hands, then it does not exist? If that were the case, nothing would exist in this world, as everything is in a constant state of flux. There would be nothing for us to discuss.

t_ch: 哈，当然！莫非你想要说，你思想中的阿卡索斯城永恒存在、真实可信，看得见摸得着的城邦，反而不如你的想象那般确定？
t_en: Hah, of course! Are you trying to claim that the Icatus in your mind will exist forever, whereas a visible and tangible city is not as much a certainty as the one in your imagination?

t_ch: 无意义的诡辩。你的提问无非是在混淆概念。
t_en: A meaningless argument. Your line of questioning is conflating different concepts with each other.

t_ch: 认为这样的讨论毫无意义，不过是思维的惰性。但实际上，整个翁法罗斯无不与之相关。
t_en: To think that such an argument lacks meaning is no more than mental laziness. In fact, this relates to everything we see in Amphoreus.

t_ch: 我们从未见过刻法勒的真身，却笃定它为奥赫玛支撑起了天地；我们不理解欧洛尼斯的话语，却执着相信命定的预言；我们都未经历过死亡，却假定塞纳托斯的存在。
t_en: We have never seen the true form of Kephale, yet we are certain that they support the world for Okhema. We do not understand the words of Oronyx, yet we stubbornly believe their prophecy. We have never experienced death, yet we assume the existence of Thanatos.

t_ch: 人总是倾向于那些宏大而虚幻的理念，又不愿去思考其中的含义。
t_en: People are always drawn to these grand and fantastic ideals, yet they are unwilling to reflect on their implications.

t_ch: 我并非不愿思考，而是不愿动摇思考的根基。
t_en: It is not that I am unwilling to reflect. I am unwilling to shake the very foundation of thought itself.

t_ch: 这位朋友，你旁听我们的论辩有段时间了，想必也是位热爱智慧的学者。有兴趣的话，不妨参与到讨论中来，让在场各位都听听你的高见？
t_en: My friend. You have overheard this dispute for some time. You must be a fellow scholar and lover of wisdom. If you are interested, why not join our discussion? We would all be interested in hearing your view.

t_ch: 让我来辩！
t_en: Let me join the debate!

t_ch: 要不你们先做个自我介绍？
t_en: How about you introduce yourselves first?

t_ch: 我是奥赫玛的希诺，以教授人们论辩的技巧为生。
t_en: I am Zeno of Okhema. I make a living by instructing people on the art of debate.

t_ch: 阿卡索斯的帕门弥德，如你所见，是位修辞学方面的教师。
t_en: Parmenides of Icatus. As you can see, I am a teacher in the study of rhetoric.

t_ch: 教师的名头太过响亮，我倒不喜欢。人们总以为能从我这学到些什么，可我能教给他们的，无非是些思考与谈话的技巧。这算得了什么？
t_en: I find the title of "teacher" overly flashy. I do not enjoy it, personally. People always think that I can teach them something, but all I can teach them are the skills of reflection and debate. Does this really count as anything?

t_ch: 照你的说法，逻辑学课堂可就一文不值了，但别忘了，你是从那毕业的。
t_en: If that is true, logic classes would be completely worthless. Let us not forget that this was your major.

t_ch: 学生的成就超过老师，是什么稀奇事吗？按你的观点，世界岂不是始终在倒退？
t_en: Is it that rare for a student to surpass their teacher? If we follow your view, surely the world would only ever be regressing?

t_ch: 好吧，现在你明白他们俩的主要工作是什么了。
t_en: Alright, now you know what their main jobs are.

t_ch: 我就是路过的。
t_en: I'm just passing by.

t_ch: 这种玩弄文辞的论辩还是少听些为好。渎神的罪名暂且不论，要是因为沉迷驳倒对方，反而损害了思考的能力，就得不偿失了。
t_en: These debates of rhetorical wordplay are best avoided. Putting aside the matter of blasphemy for now, if you end up damaging your brain trying to refute your opposition's argument, the victory is still not worth the loss.

t_ch: 哎，辩论才是获取真理的方式。不经由讨论便得到的知识，与盲信有什么区别？
t_en: Ah, but debate is the only method to obtain the truth. Is there any difference between knowledge that has not undergone discussion and blind faith?

t_ch: 眼见两人又要为「真理」的定义争吵起来，你赶忙快步离开了现场。
t_en: Seeing that both of the men before you are hurtling towards another argument about the definition of "truth," you rush away from the scene as quickly as possible.

t_ch: 阿卡索斯的帕门弥德，我要继续刚才的发问。
t_en: Parmenides of Icatus, I wish to continue my questioning from before.

t_ch: 奥赫玛识字的公民都知道，阿卡索斯早已在黑潮中覆灭。你如何从一座不存在的城邦来？
t_en: As all who can read in Okhema know that Icatus was long since destroyed by the black tide. How could you have come from a city that does not exist?

t_ch: 是我先前的表述有误。我曾来自阿卡索斯，那是我的故乡，黑潮来临前，我携带所有财产逃来了奥赫玛。我来自尚未覆灭的阿卡索斯，这点千真万确。
t_en: I made a mistake in my last remark. I originally come from Icatus, and that is my home. Before the black tide descended, I took all of my worldly belongings and fled to Okhema. I come from an Icatus that has not yet been destroyed. This point is absolutely true.

t_ch: 这位朋友，你旁听我们的论辩有段时间了，想必也是位热爱智慧的学者。有兴趣的话，不妨参与到讨论中来，让在场各位都听听你的高见？
t_en: My friend. You have overheard this dispute for some time. You must be a fellow scholar and lover of wisdom. If you are interested, why not join our discussion? We would all be interested in hearing your view.

t_ch: 既然这位智者愿意加入讨论，我们就来选个直观而深奥的论题。阿卡索斯的帕门弥德，我先退让一步，将确定论题的权力交付你。
t_en: As this wise one has seen fit to join us in debate, let us select a topic that is both intuitive and profound. Parmenides of Icatus, I shall step back and pass the rights of topic selection to you.

t_ch: 好，依我看，我们不妨继续刚才的辩论。理念与现实，究竟何者更为可靠？
t_en: Very well. I think that we may as well continue the debate that we were having before: Ideals or reality, which is more reliable?

t_ch: 将其转换为更直接的论题，便是——<color=#dbc291ff>纯粹的「阿卡索斯」是否真实存在？</color>
t_en: If we were to convert it into a more direct topic, it would be: <color=#dbc291ff>Does a pure "Icatus" truly exist?</color>

t_ch: 很好的论题。我得说，这个疑问困惑过我很长的时间。被黑潮摧毁后的阿卡索斯城看不见，摸不着，即使去到原先的地点，也只能找到一堆断壁残垣。
t_en: A very fine topic. I must say, this question has troubled me for a long time. Having been devoured by the black tide, one can no longer see nor touch Icatus. Even if you go to its location, all you will see is a pile of ruined remains.

t_ch: 就算在覆灭前，城邦的面积与人口也在不断发生变化，你童年时见到的城邦，和青年时见到的必然不同。既然如此，你凭什么断定它就是阿卡索斯？
t_en: Even before its destruction, the area and population of the city were undergoing constant change. The city that you saw as a child was undoubtedly different to the one you saw as a youth. If that is the case, how can you conclude that it was indeed Icatus?

t_ch: 秉着简洁的原则，我认为——<color=#dbc291ff>「阿卡索斯」并不存在</color>，能确定的只是*有个城邦*，仅此而已。
t_en: Based on the principle of simplicity, I think that... "Icatus" does not in fact exist, and all we can be certain of is that there is a city.

t_ch: 我与你恰好相反。「阿卡索斯」不会因为覆灭而消失，我们依旧可以谈论它、怀念它，就好像它仍是个真实存在的城邦似的。
t_en: I believe precisely the opposite. Icatus does not simply stop existing because it was destroyed. We can still discuss it and reminisce on it as if it were a city that still truly exists.

t_ch: 当我提起「阿卡索斯」的时候，所有人都知道说的是什么。当包裹寄往「阿卡索斯」的时候，信使绝不会弄错目的地。是我的故乡，这点永远不会改变。
t_en: When I mention "Icatus," everyone knows what that is. When you send a parcel to "Icatus," there is no way that the messenger would go to the wrong location. It is my hometown. That will never change.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「阿卡索斯」必然存在</color>，否则当我们谈论「阿卡索斯」的时候，为何能理解彼此想表达的内容？
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>"Icatus" must exist</color>. Otherwise, when we discuss "Icatus," how could we possibly understand what each other is referring to?

t_ch: 我们双方都阐述了各自的立场。那么，这位智者，你要选择为哪方辩护？
t_en: We have both stated our view points. So, wise one, which side would you like to debate for?

t_ch: 我同意希诺的观点。
t_en: I agree with Zeno's view.

t_ch: 异乡的智者啊，莫要被诡辩术迷惑了你的思考。跟从感官的结果固然轻松，但别忘了，理性才是灵魂的主宰。我将为你讲述我的见解。
t_en: Wise one from elsewhere, do not allow your mind to be clouded by sophistry. Though it is easy to follow one's senses, do not forget that reason is the master of the soul. Allow me to explain my understanding.

t_ch: 请听。我们所有的知识，都从存在的事物中来。将军看到阿卡索斯，会思考该如何在城墙上布防；农夫看到阿卡索斯，会盘算这里的土壤适合种什么作物。
t_en: Listen. All of our knowledge comes from objects in existence. When a general sees Icatus, they will think of how to post defenses along the city walls. When a farmer sees Icatus, they will assess what kind of crops are suited to the earth at that locale.

t_ch: 学者看到阿卡索斯，会联想起它悠久辉煌的历史。但我们都清楚，他们脑海里的阿卡索斯，绝不是「阿卡索斯」，总是在这方面或那方面有些差别。
t_en: When a scholar sees Icatus, they will think of its long and glorious history. However, we understand clearly that the Icatus in their minds cannot be "Icatus." It is always different depending on which aspect they are looking at.

t_ch: 那么，若不是我们已经有了关于「阿卡索斯」的知识，怎么能判断出他们的观点不够全面？若是「阿卡索斯」不存在，我们能从何处得到的关于它的知识？
t_en: So, if we did not already have knowledge of "Icatus," how could we realize that their views are not comprehensive enough? If "Icatus" does not exist, from where did we gain our knowledge of it?

t_ch: 不错，漂亮的三段论。知识必须从存在的事物中来，我们拥有关于「阿卡索斯」的知识，因此可以推断出「阿卡索斯」必然存在。
t_en: Not bad. Textbook syllogism. Knowledge must come from things that exist. We have knowledge of "Icatus," therefore, we can deduce that "Icatus" must exist.

t_ch: 但这个逻辑并非无懈可击。异乡的智者啊，不知你有没有看到其中的疏漏？
t_en: However, this logic is not infallible. Wise one from outlands, I wonder if you have seen the flaw in this?

t_ch: （质疑：大前提有误。）
t_en: (Question: The main premise is flawed.)

t_ch: 知识并非必须从存在的事物中来，只有依靠感官获得的知识才是如此。你刚才的推论中，已经蕴含了反面例证。
t_en: Knowledge does not have to come from things that exist. Only knowledge that comes from the senses is obtained this way. You already mentioned an example that disproves this in your previous argument.

t_ch: 数学，毫无疑问是公认的学科，可关于数学的任何公理都无法从存在的事物中找到。你永远见不到真正的圆形，却不影响我们谈论「圆」的知识。
t_en: Mathematics is undeniably a recognized school, yet there is no self-evident truth of mathematics that can be found within objects that exist. You will never see a true circle, yet that does not stop us from discussing knowledge regarding "circles."

t_ch: 逻辑学同样如此。既然使用三段论来论证你的观点，难道你还要否认逻辑规律并不是知识吗？
t_en: The study of logic is much the same. As you have used syllogism to prove your point, surely you will not deny that the rules of logic are not true knowledge?

t_ch: 哈！智者的驳论真是毫不留情面。帕门弥德，现在你可得好好想想咯。
t_en: Hah! What a merciless rebuttal, wise one! You will have to think about this carefully, Parmenides.

t_ch: （质疑：小前提有误。）
t_en: (Question: Your minor premise is flawed.)

t_ch: 我们真的拥有关于「阿卡索斯」的知识吗？从未接受过数学训练的孩童也能轻松辨认出圆形，并准确地描述出「黎明机器」更大，车盖更小。
t_en: Do we truly have knowledge of "Icatus"? A child that has never been educated in mathematics can easily identify a circle, and accurately explain that the "Dawn Device" is larger than a vehicle cover.

t_ch: 难道数学家的知识，和那些小孩子是相同的吗？要真是这样的话，修辞学也不存在了，人们直接在集市里便能学到所有的论辩知识。
t_en: Are you saying that the knowledge of a mathematician is equal to the knowledge of such children? If that is in fact the case, then the study of rhetoric also does not exist. People can learn everything they need with regard to debate at their local market.

t_ch: 哈！智者的话语真是一阵见血。帕门弥德，你怕是要砸掉自己的饭碗咯。
t_en: Hah! Your words go right for the throat, wise one! Parmenides, you are in danger of destroying your own profession!

t_ch: （质疑：结论有误。）
t_en: (Question: The conclusion is flawed.)

t_ch: 「阿卡索斯」当然不存在。你这个结论本身就是错误的。
t_en: Of course "Icatus" does not exist. Your very conclusion is incorrect.

t_ch: 所以是为何呢？跳过全部推导过程，直接否认结果，这样的质疑怕是构不成你的驳论。
t_en: And for what reason? I fear that circumventing the process of deduction and directly refuting my conclusion do not, in fact, constitute a rebuttal.

t_ch: 果然，我早猜到这样的推论无法蒙混过关。但异乡的智者，你刚才的驳斥也为我提供了新的论证思路。
t_en: I knew that it would not be so simple as just presenting my deduction without justification. However, your rebuttal just now has presented me with a new line of thought, wise one from beyond.

t_ch: 数学是最为简洁优美的学科，数学的公理无论何时都不会改变。即便有哪天，现实中找不到任何圆形的东西，「圆」也仍旧存在。
t_en: Mathematics is the most simple and beautiful of studies. Its innate truths will never change. Even if there comes a day when there is no such thing as a circular object in existence, the concept of a "circle" will still exist.

t_ch: 「阿卡索斯」正是如此。看不见、摸不着，只能通过我们的理性来认知，规定着所有能找到的阿卡索斯城。可它确实存在，就在某个地方，并且永远不会消亡。
t_en: "Icatus" is much the same. It cannot be seen nor touched. It is only recognized through our rational cognition, which regulates all the cities of Icatus that we find. However, it does in fact exist somewhere, and it will never disappear.

t_ch: 若是否定「阿卡索斯」，就相当于推翻所有数学知识，智者可要考虑清楚了。
t_en: If we deny the existence of "Icatus," we overturn all knowledge of mathematics. This is something that you must consider carefully, wise one.

t_ch: （驳斥：你在混淆概念。）
t_en: (Rebuttal: You are confusing separate concepts.)

t_ch: 这两者并不能简单等同。数学知识在没有人的世界中仍然能成立，可「阿卡索斯」呢？若是没有最初的那批建城者，这个城邦便会变得从未存在过。
t_en: You cannot equate these two concepts so simply. The truths of mathematics would still stand to reason in a world where humans never existed, but what of Icatus? If it weren't for its original builders, the city would never have existed in the first place.

t_ch: 数学是阐述必然性的知识，哪怕悬锋城的战士全部改信墨涅塔，圆依旧是圆，不会变成方形。但「阿卡索斯」的存在，必须依赖现实历史。
t_en: Mathematics explains absolute and certain truth. If all the warriors of Castrum Kremnos were to suddenly follow Mnestia, a circle would still be a circle. It would not change shape. However, the existence of "Icatus" absolutely relies on real history.

t_ch: 要是你的先祖当时得到了某种神启，将城邦换个名字，「阿卡索斯」就不再存在，怎么能说这个概念可以和数学知识相类比呢？
t_en: If your ancestors had received divine inspiration and changed the name of the city, "Icatus" would not exist. How can you say that this concept is comparable to the truths of mathematics?

t_ch: 即便退一步，认可「阿卡索斯」确实是知识，它也绝非数学那样的必然知识。帕门弥德，这个例证你可用得太不恰当了。
t_en: Even if we take a step back and accept that we do in fact have knowledge of "Icatus," how can we say that this knowledge is similar to the absolute knowledge of mathematics? Parmenides, this example is not a fitting comparison.

t_ch: （驳斥：你在虚设实体。）
t_en: (Rebuttal: You are creating a fictional entity.)

t_ch: 要是「阿卡索斯」真实存在，请问「奥赫玛」与「悬锋城」是否也该真实存在？「神悟树庭」与「哀丽秘榭」是否也真实存在？
t_en: If "Icatus" truly exists, do "Okhema" and "Castrum Kremnos" also truly exist as well? What about "Grove of Epiphany" and "Aedes Elysiae"?

t_ch: 假使有位好事的吟游诗人，在诗歌中虚构了某座城邦，并借助自己的名声让所有人都相信，这就是远古曾存在过的地点，只不过现在被摧毁了。
t_en: What if there were a great bard that created a fictional city in their songs and used their fame to convince everyone that it really existed, and it was just destroyed long ago?

t_ch: 那么这座虚构的城邦，是否也有对应的的「概念」？毕竟，按你的说法，阿卡索斯能否找到并不重要，你思想中的那座城邦才更为真实可信。
t_en: Does this fictional city then become the same kind of "concept"? After all, according to your argument, it is not whether one can locate Icatus that matters. To you, it is the concept of the city that you have in your mind that is more credible and true.

t_ch: 但谁能保证我们的想法，不是扎格列斯开的某个玩笑呢？
t_en: But who can ensure that our thoughts and beliefs are not simply just a trick played on us by Zagreus?

t_ch: 欧洛尼斯执掌记忆权柄，完全有能力虚构你的过往。帕门弥德，别太认真了。
t_en: Oronyx holds power over our memories and is completely capable of fabricating our past. Parmenides, you must not place too much weight on such things.

t_ch: （驳斥：你在循环论证。）
t_en: (Rebuttal: You are making a circular argument.)

t_ch: 我们看到了圆形的东西，才得到「圆」的概念。「阿卡索斯」也是这样，但现在你却说，「阿卡索斯」规定着所有能找到的阿卡索斯城。
t_en: I have to see a circular object to obtain the concept of a "circle." "Icatus" is the same. However, you are now saying that "Icatus" itself defines the cities of Icatus that we discover for ourselves.

t_ch: 这难道不是倒果为因，陷入了无限循环的推论模式吗？
t_en: Are you not confusing cause and effect and creating an endless loop of inference?

t_ch: 智者啊，你似乎到现在都未明白我的论点。「圆」的概念起初便在我们的思想中了，否则该如何说明我们从未见过完美的圆，却能在脑海中构想出来？
t_en: Wise one, it seems that you still have not grasped my point. The concept of a "circle" was in our minds from the very beginning. Otherwise, how do we explain our ability to imagine a perfect circle despite never having seen one?

t_ch: 「圆」的知识先于现实的圆形东西而存在，「阿卡索斯」同样如此。
t_en: Knowledge of "circles" existed before real objects that are round. "Icatus" is the same.

t_ch: 就到这里吧，智者连续两次驳倒了我的论证，无话可说，确实是位厉害的辩论家。以后还请多与我们交流你的观点。
t_en: Let us leave the argument here for now. You have successfully rebuked my arguments twice in a row now, wise one. I have no choice but to admit that you are a great debater. I hope that you will continue to share your perspectives with us in the future.

t_ch: 哈！看起来，你似乎还未完全被说服？
t_en: Hah! I see that you are still not entirely convinced?

t_ch: 论辩不过是言语间的游戏，得不出真理，更无法改变信念。
t_en: The art of debate is simply no more than a game of words. There is no truth to be gained, nor can it change my beliefs.

t_ch: 我反对你的立场，并非因为坚信「阿卡索斯」真实存在，而是这些概念对知识的传承至关重要。若是按你的做法，对任何事物都抱着否定态度……
t_en: My opposition to your view is not because I staunchly believe in the existence of "Icatus." I disagree because these concepts are crucial in the inheritance of knowledge. If we follow your approach and maintain an attitude of denial towards everything...

t_ch: 还有谁会去探究真理，改变世界？怀疑看似绝对正确，却无法带来任何收益。为了向前走，为了更好的生活，我们需要信念。
t_en: Who would still go in search of truth and transform the world? Though suspicion can seem absolutely certain, it cannot bring us any benefits at all. We require faith to keep moving forward and create a better life.

t_ch: 并且…若是「阿卡索斯」不存在了，我…阿卡索斯的帕门弥德，该回哪去呢？
t_en: What's more... If "Icatus" were no longer to exist... Then where would I, Parmenides of Icatus, have to return to?

t_ch: 我同意帕门弥德的观点。
t_en: I agree with Parmenides.

t_ch: 哈，我就猜到你准会相信他那套言论。要是在奥赫玛公然质疑阿卡索斯的真实性，是要引起众怒的。但没关系，我愿意为我的论点辩护。
t_en: Hah, I knew that you would favor his argument. If Okhema were to publicly doubt the truth of Icatus' existence, it would be certain to incite outrage. I do not mind, however. I am willing to defend my theory.

t_ch: 听好了。「阿卡索斯」无法借由感官认知，我们也缺少观察它的方式，无非是人们通过想象编造出的谎言。没有任何实际证据，你该如何证明它的存在？
t_en: Listen. Icatus cannot be known through our senses, and we lack any method of observing it. It is no more than a lie created by the imagination of mankind. If there is no real existing evidence, how can you prove its existence?

t_ch: （质疑：论点有误。）
t_en: (Question: Your theory is flawed.)

t_ch: 无法被感官认知的事物就必定不存在吗？盲人看不见你的样貌，耳聋的人听不见你的话语，难道就能因此推断出你并不存在？
t_en: If we cannot detect something through our senses, does that absolutely deny its existence? If a blind person cannot see your face and a deaf person cannot hear your words, can we then infer that you do not actually exist?

t_ch: 若是有座城邦，远在翁法罗斯的另一头，从未与奥赫玛有过任何通信，按你刚才的论点，那座城邦的人们便全都消失不见了。
t_en: If there were another city on the other side of Amphoreus that had never had contact with Okhema, then according to your logic, the people of this city would disappear without a trace.

t_ch: 诡辩是好奇心与探索精神的大敌，要是将知识局限在感官接触的范围内，那我们永远都无法走出奥赫玛。
t_en: Sophistry is the enemy of both curiosity and the spirit of exploration. If we limited knowledge to what we can sense, we would never have been able to set foot outside Okhema.

t_ch: （质疑：论证方式有误。）
t_en: (Question: Your means of refutation is flawed.)

t_ch: 抛开事实不谈，你的论证方式本身就有问题。想要证明某个事物存在，只用找到一个证据就够了，即便现在没有，也无法否认未来可能会出现。
t_en: Putting the facts aside, your method of proof itself is flawed. If you wish to prove the existence of something, you only need a single piece of evidence. Even if that evidence is not presently available, you cannot deny that it could appear at some point in the future.

t_ch: 但想要证明某个事物不存在，需要从根本上进行否定，无论现在或未来，都没办法找到任何证据。因此，这并非事实层面的不存在，而是概念层面的不可能。
t_en: However, if you wish to prove that something does not exist, you have to fundamentally deny the possibility — Indicate that there is no possible way for us to ever find evidence of its existence, whether in the present or the future. Therefore, this does not equate to non-existence on a factual level. It equates to conceptual impossibility.

t_ch: 概念的不可能，无法通过事实来推断。
t_en: Conceptual impossibility cannot be deduced through reality.

t_ch: 缺乏证据，只能推导出命题不能被证实，却无法证伪该命题。你该回去再跟着老师学学逻辑课了。
t_en: If there is a lack of evidence, the only possible deduction is that the proposition cannot be verified, but it also cannot be falsified. You should head back for more logic classes with your teacher.

t_ch: （论证：「阿卡索斯」确实存在。）
t_en: (Theory: "Icatus" does actually exist.)

t_ch: 「阿卡索斯」当然存在，地图上标注着城邦的位置，吟游诗人传颂着城邦的故事，奥赫玛里无数流民都将向你证明它的真实存在。
t_en: Of course "Icatus" exists. The city's location being marked on maps, bards singing of stories from the city, and countless refugees coming to Okhema all prove its existence.

t_ch: 不错。我便是其中之一。
t_en: That's right. I am one of those refugees myself.

t_ch: 哈，哲学辩论的结果，岂是由人证的多少来决定的？若真是如此，公民大会就将是这世上最富有智慧的机构——他们永远都是对的。
t_en: Hah. So a philosophical conclusion is proven simply by the number of people that believe it? If that were the case, then the citizens' assembly would be the most intelligent organization on earth... They would always be correct.

t_ch: 你的这番言论，属实有些太过欠缺思考了。还是仔细想想吧。
t_en: These remarks of yours require further reflection. Why not think about this a little more?

t_ch: 不错。异乡的智者果然精通辩论，你成功抓住了这番话里的薄弱环节，但还没有彻底击倒我。我将继续我的论证。
t_en: Not bad. You excel in the art of debate — as expected, wise one from outlands. You have successfully grasped the weaknesses within my assertions. However, you have not completely refuted my argument. Allow me to continue my point.

t_ch: 若是「阿卡索斯」真实存在，请问——它到底以什么方式存在？
t_en: If "Icatus" truly exists, then allow me to ask you... In what form does it exist?

t_ch: 要是它在时间之内存在，那么它就处于流变当中。就连泰坦都会陷入疯狂，就连尼卡多利都会陨落，时间内的事物，该如何永久存在？
t_en: If it exists in time, then it is in a state of flow. Even the Titans will descend into madness and Nikador will fall with time. How can anything in time exist forever?

t_ch: 要是它在空间之内存在，那么它必然有固定形状。它是圆形的、方形的、和现在的阿卡索斯城相同的，还是奇形怪状，根本无法被度量？
t_en: If it exists in space, then it must have a physical form. Is it circular? Square? Is it the same shape as Icatus is in currently? Or is it another obscure shape that cannot be measured?

t_ch: 要是它在时空外存在，那么我实在想不出能以什么方式来认识它。观察依赖光线，发声需要时间，文字借助形状，时空外存在的事物，是如何被你知晓的？
t_en: If it exists outside of space-time, then I truly cannot think of what method I can use to gain knowledge of it. Observation relies on light, sound requires time, and words make use of shape... How can that which exists outside of space-time ever be known?

t_ch: 异乡的智者，请解答我的疑惑——
t_en: Wise one from beyond, please enlighten me...

t_ch: （论证：是通过认知得到的。）
t_en: (Theory: We learn through our cognition.)

t_ch: 居住在阿卡索斯的人们，自然对城邦有着认识，任何来到过、见过或是听说过阿卡索斯的人，也会形成对应的认识。
t_en: Those who live in Icatus will naturally have a cognition of it. Anyone who has been to, seen, or heard of Icatus, will help form the corresponding cognition.

t_ch: 这些认识经过讲述与传承，便成为了关于「阿卡索斯」的知识。
t_en: This cognition is described to and inherited by others to become knowledge of "Icatus."

t_ch: 很浪漫的想法，但你似乎并未理解我的发问。人的认知并不可靠，经过转达与记录的信息更加真假莫辨。虚假的东西再怎么叠加，也得不到真实。
t_en: A very romantic notion, but it seems that you have not understood my question. The cognition of mankind is not reliable. It becomes even harder to ascertain the verity or falsity of any information that has been passed on or left in record. No matter how many falsehoods have piled up, it does not make them truths.

t_ch: （论证：是通过思考得到的。）
t_en: (Theory: We learn through thinking.)

t_ch: 假使有位学者生活在阿卡索斯，对这座城邦有着充分的了解，他知道城市的面积、人口、统治者、全部历史，甚至所有街道的模样。
t_en: Imagine there was a scholar who lived in Icatus. He has a deep understanding of the city and knows its area, population, rulers, all of its history, and even every street and road.

t_ch: 经过详尽的思考，他得出「阿卡索斯」的知识，并教给了其他人。
t_en: Through detailed reflection, he obtains knowledge of "Icatus" and teaches it to others.

t_ch: 很超脱的想法，但你似乎并未理解我的发问。就算那位学者拥有瑟希斯的智慧，他所观察到的也仍旧是那时那刻，从他的视角出发的阿卡索斯。
t_en: An unconventional line of thought, but it seems that you have not understood my question. Even if that scholar had the intellect of Cerces, he would only be able to observe the Icatus from his point of view at that moment.

t_ch: 以偏概全是知识的大忌，没有人能将自身的想法置于其余人之上。
t_en: Generalization is a taboo when it comes to speaking of knowledge. No one can impose their thoughts on others.

t_ch: （质疑：前提有误。）
t_en: (Question: Your premise is flawed.)

t_ch: 时间之外，当然有物存在。我们度量时间，能从一数到十，正是因为在数到后面的数时，前面的数并未消失。有数在先，才能有数在后。
t_en: Of course some objects exist outside of time. When we measure time, we can count from one to ten precisely because the earlier numbers still exist by the time I have reached the later numbers. I must count the earlier numbers to reach the latter.

t_ch: 空间之外，同样有物存在。我们衡量空间，必然是测算物体间的距离，比较物体间的位置。我们只能说某物离某物近，某物在某物的旁边。
t_en: There are also objects that exist outside of space. When we measure space, we have to measure the distance between objects and compare their locations in space. We can only say that something is close to something else, or that something is beside something else.

t_ch: 离开在先的数，时间将无法被度量；离开作为参照的某物，空间将无法被衡量。那么，时空究竟是囊括所有事物的盒子，还是依附事物才有意义的东西？
t_en: Time cannot be measured without numbers preceding it. Space cannot be measured without an object to serve as a reference. So, does that mean that space-time is a giant box that encapsulates all things, or is it something that only has meaning by attaching itself to something else?

t_ch: 无论作何解释，时空都不能成为否定事物存在的理由。
t_en: No matter which explanation you choose, space-time cannot serve as a reason to deny the existence of something.

t_ch: 精彩！智者的论辩果然高明，若是你参加公民大会，必定能成为正义的代言人吧。
t_en: Wonderful! Your ability to debate is truly brilliant! If you were to join the citizens' assembly, I am certain that you could become an ambassador of justice!

t_ch: 我暂时想不出新的论点，是你们获胜了。但还请注意，我输了，并非意味着你们的立场便是正确的。说到底，辩论无非是驳倒对方的游戏，至于真理……
t_en: I cannot currently think of a new argument, so victory is yours. However, please be aware that my loss does not mean that your view is absolutely correct. In the end, debate is simply no more than a game of refuting the other party's arguments. When it comes to the truth...

t_ch: 谁知道它是否存在呢？我能做的，无非是提醒人们对未经思考的事物保持怀疑。无论那是阿卡索斯，是泰坦神明，亦或是…绝对正确的预言。
t_en: Who knows if it exists? All I can do is remind people to remain suspicious of any object that has not been thoroughly reflected upon. Whether that be Icatus, the Titans and gods, or even... A prophecy of absolute truth.

t_ch: 世界永远不可知——唯有怀疑，以及我正在怀疑，才是确定无疑的事。
t_en: The world can never be fully known — the only things that are free of doubt are doubt itself, and the fact that I am doubting.

t_ch: 不，不对...这里的阴影应该更深一些。啊，但如果加深，会不会影响整体的平衡？真是令人困扰...这种灰色总是让我想起雨后的天空，但它又应该更...更澄澈些。
t_en: No, that's off... these shadows should be deeper. Though, darkening them too much might mess up the balance. It's tricky... this gray reminds me of the sky after a storm, but it needs to be crisper.

t_ch: 嗯？抱歉，我没注意到有人来了。你也对这面墙感兴趣吗？
t_en: Oops, sorry, I didn't notice you there. Are you interested in this wall too?

t_ch: 不，我只是被你吸引了。
t_en: No, I was just drawn to you.

t_ch: 真的吗？刚才我在观察我的壁画作品，有些太投入了，可能吓到你了，抱歉。
t_en: Really? I was so engrossed in observing my artwork, that I might have startled you, sorry.

t_ch: 不，我只是路过。
t_en: No, I was just passing by.

t_ch: 原来如此，我还以为你也是来欣赏壁画的呢。
t_en: I see. I thought you were here to appreciate the mural.

t_ch: 相逢即是雅努斯的祝福，身后这幅壁画是我的作品，不知你对壁画是否感兴趣——我想听听你的意见。
t_en: Our chance encounter must be blessed by Janus. This mural behind me is my creation. I'm curious if you have an interest in murals — I'd love to hear your thoughts.

t_ch: 呃，我不太懂这些。
t_en: I don't know much about these things.

t_ch: 没事，你若是不嫌弃我啰嗦，我正好可以为你介绍一二。
t_en: No worries. If you don't mind my chatter, I can introduce you to a thing or two.

t_ch: 算了吧，我对这些不感兴趣。
t_en: Hold it right there. I'm not interested in this stuff.

t_ch: 这样啊，有点可惜…不过这种事，倒也不好勉强你，都怪我太冒昧了。
t_en: That's a pity... but I shouldn't impose. It was presumptuous of me.

t_ch: 我正在尝试用不同深浅的灰色来表现天空的光照变化，艾格勒的仁慈赋予了奥赫玛如此美妙的景象。
t_en: I'm experimenting with various shades of gray to capture the shifting light in the sky. Aquila's mercy has blessed Okhema with such breathtaking scenery.

t_ch: 你看，这里的深灰代表阴影，而这里的浅灰色则是光照最强的地方。我知道常人眼中可能不是这样，但对我来说，这就是世界的样子。
t_en: You see, the deep gray here symbolizes the shadows, and the lighter gray marks the areas with the brightest light. I know it might not appear this way to everyone else, but this is how I see the world.

t_ch: 灰色的…天空？
t_en: A gray... sky?

t_ch: 冒昧问下，你看不见颜色吗？
t_en: May I ask... can you not see colors?

t_ch: 嗯，我不太能分清颜色。小时候我曾和常人无异，直到…家乡被黑潮席卷，我的眼睛就是在那里被侵蚀的。
t_en: I can't really distinguish colors anymore. I was just like anyone else as a child until the black tide swept through my hometown and corroded my eyes.

t_ch: …唔，你不必露出这么悲伤的表情，我曾经因为自己的缺陷而感到自卑，直到阿格莱雅大人偶然点拨了我几句。
t_en: Don't look so sad. I once struggled with my flaws until Lady Aglaea shared wisdom that changed my perspective.

t_ch: 她的话让我以不同的方式看待这个世界，反而能更直观地感受到身边的情感波动。
t_en: Her words shifted my perspective on the world, enabling me to perceive emotional fluctuations more directly.

t_ch: 虽然我看不到常人眼中的湛蓝色的穹顶，但我能感受到它…对我来说，那是一种...深沉而柔和的灰色。宁静，又带着一丝神秘。
t_en: Though I can't see the azure dome like others, I can feel it... To me, it appears as a deep, gentle gray — calm and subtly mysterious.

t_ch: 你可能会觉得奇怪，为什么天空是灰色的？但在我的世界里，情感和色彩是紧密相连的。温暖、宁静、期待...这些感觉在我眼中就是不同深浅的灰色。
t_en: You might find it strange why the sky is gray, but in my world, emotions and colors are closely linked. Warmth, calmness, anticipation... these feelings are different shades of gray to me.

t_ch: 因祸得福，我在壁画领域也算是小有名气…唔，忘了自我介绍了，我叫福尔图娜，很高兴认识你。
t_en: A blessing in disguise, I've made a name for myself in the mural field... uh, forgot to introduce myself. I'm Fortuna, nice to meet you.

t_ch: 灰色的天空？听起来很有趣。
t_en: A gray sky? Sounds intriguing.

t_ch: 是的！你懂我的意思吗？艺术不应该局限于我们的视觉。它应该突破这些界限，直击人心。我努力用自己的方式感受这个世界，然后把它呈现出来。
t_en: Yes! Do you get what I mean? Art shouldn't be confined to our vision. It ought to transcend those limits and resonate deeply with us. I strive to experience the world in my unique way and then express it through my art.

t_ch: 比如说，当我画奥赫玛的集市时，我不会去关注水果的颜色，而是专注于集市的喧嚣、人们的欢笑、讨价还价的热闹。
t_en: Take, for instance, when I paint the marketplace in Okhema, I don't focus on the colors of the fruit. Instead, I capture the buzz of the crowd, their laughter, and the vibrant bartering.
